text: Year of the World 3414, Year before Christ 590.
Sedecias. He was less impious than his two predecessors; but too weak to do good.
Pray, or consult. He wished to know whether the Chaldeans would return. (Calmet)<hr>He feared, yet ill-treated the prophet, as Herod did John the Baptist. (Worthington)

Return, routed, Jeremias 46:15. His army was small, Ezechiel 17:17., and 30:21. This king Ephree, (chap. 44:30.) or Apries, had succeeded Psammis, after an interregnum of ten years' continuance. (Herodotus 2:161.)<hr>He foresaw that his own dominions would be invaded, after Jerusalem, the key, was taken. The other allies of Juda perfidiously sat still, or joined the Chaldeans. (Calmet)
Away. They departed indeed, but for a short time.
Tent. So easy it is for God to destroy by few as well as by many! (Haydock)

Possession, lately purchased, Jeremias 32. (Lyranus)<hr>Yet some think this had not yet taken place. (Calmet)<hr>Septuagint, "to buy provisions among." Hebrew, "to slip away thence in the midst of the people." (Haydock)<hr>He wished to escape the fury of his enemies at Jerusalem. (Calmet)<hr>It is an old device of persecutors to lay false charges on the innocent, as Julian and the Arians did. (Hist. Tripart. 6:27.) (Worthington)
Beat him, as a traitor and fugitive.<hr>Prison, very deep, ver. 19. (Calmet)
Prison. Literally, "lake or pit," (Haydock) such as was used to keep wine. Thus God tries his chosen friends, according to their strength!
Prophets. He insults over these impostors, and speaks boldly. The Chaldeans were now returned, Jeremias 38.

Entry, where he had been already, Jeremias 32:1., and 34:1, 7.<hr>Piece. Roll, sufficient for a day's maintenance. (Calmet)<hr>Protestants, "a piece of bread (Septuagint, a loaf) out of the baker's street." (Haydock)
